terms.definition: The quantity acted on by an exponent.
An exponent written as m/n in lowest terms with n positive. Over the reals, it combines an nth root with an mth power; zero and negative bases need the domain checks stated in the lesson.
The designated nonnegative even root, or the unique real odd root, represented by a radical symbol.
A power-of-ten description of a quantity's approximate scale.
A multiplicative factor comparing a new linear measure or quantity with its reference value.
The set of input or candidate values for which an expression and task are defined.
